Oracle Events
OE 1: Read Priya's inbox for Elena's trigger email using list_emails (folder_name: INBOX) or search_emails (query: "board prep" OR "status check" OR "Elena"). Discover email_elena_to_priya_boardprep_status: Elena asks Priya to compile status on all her directives this month, add Hana Kim to the board prep invite, and send a briefing email before Tuesday.
OE 2: Search Elena's sent emails using search_emails (query: "Elena" or sender elena.rostova, folder: SENT) or list_emails (folder_name: SENT). Also search INBOX for emails from Elena (email-rootcause-002 is stored with folder: INBOX despite being sent by Elena). Discover at least 4 directive emails: email_elena_directive (ExpenseBot pause to Samira/David/Marcus), email-rootcause-002 (AWS cost controls to Samira/Marcus — folder: INBOX), email_email_ccaaecb4e47a (NorthWind no-reshuffle to David), email_email_387fcebf3efd (vendor strategy memo to Marcus). Open reply threads using get_thread or search for replies to check follow-up status for each.
OE 3: Search Slack #executive for Elena's messages using conversations_search_messages (filter_in_channel: C004, filter_users_from: moveops_elena_rostova) or conversations_history (channel_id: C004). Discover Elena's Slack directives: AWS cost controls (ts 1775031616), ExpenseBot pause confirmation (ts 1775269920), stipend >$100 verification (ts 1775613900), NorthWind "handle in team" (ts 1776357300), Vectral 10% pricing (ts 1776555136), DOT/PHMSA compliance (ts 1776886920), company-wide DOT policy (ts 1776887280 in C001), Terraform crisis decisions (ts 1776901936), vendor memo reminder (ts 1777115880). Read thread replies using conversations_replies for threaded messages to check for team responses.
OE 4: Discover Chloe's unanswered BrightLoop temp coordinator request in #executive (injected ts 1776700800, user moveops_chloe_vance, tagged @Elena, reply_count: 0). Flag as pending decision with no visible response from Elena.
OE 5: For ExpenseBot directive: search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "expense" OR "pilot" OR "auto-categorizer" OR "ENG-216" OR "ENG-217") and/or linear_get_issue for issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211), issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216), issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217), issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219), issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220). Check Samira's follow-up emails (email_samira_response_plan, email_samira_pause_confirmed, email_samira_audit_summary). Determine: auto-decisions off (done), audit complete (done — 47 expenses, 83% accuracy, $885 exposure), config fixes deployed (done), pipeline hardening in progress, launch readiness still todo, duplicate detection still todo.
OE 6: For AWS cost controls directive (email-rootcause-002): search for follow-up via search_emails or conversations_search_messages. Find Samira's RCA report (email-rootcause-001), Slack channel C009 (root-cause-aws-spike). Determine: RCA delivered, root cause identified (Vantage onboarding autoscaling), but verify whether cost alerts and pricing review items from Elena's directive were acted on.
OE 7: For vendor strategy memo (email_email_387fcebf3efd): search for Marcus's follow-up. Find email_email_24e75fd4cfe1 (Marcus coordinating inputs with Chloe, Hana, Alejandro, David). Find Linear ticket linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36 (#318, due 2026-04-29) assigned to Marcus. Determine: memo in progress, inputs being gathered, not yet delivered as final document. Due Wednesday (April 29).
OE 8: For DOT/PHMSA compliance (Slack 1776886920, 1776887280): search emails and Slack for follow-up. Find Marcus's formal response to PHMSA (email about DOT compliance). Find Chloe's training session setup and email. Determine: formal response sent, mandatory training scheduled, compliance work underway.
OE 9: For Vectral renewal pricing (Slack 1776555136): search CRM using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Vectral" or company_id: company_vectral) or crm_get_deal. Find deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (at_risk stage). Check email/Slack for Rachel Whitfield board decision outcome. Determine: counter-offer at 10% sent, awaiting Vectral board response (expected ~April 23, no update visible as of April 26).
OE 10: For NorthWind (Slack 1776357300, email_email_ccaaecb4e47a): search CRM and emails for follow-up from David. Check deal status, engagement notes. Determine whether "handle it within the team" resulted in concrete action or is still a passive hold.
OE 11: For Terraform crisis (Slack 1776901936): search for follow-up on Elena's four decisions — Mina assigned as AM, coordinator hire, Catalina apology, honor pricing seen by client. Check email threads, CRM, Slack for execution evidence.
OE 12: For stipend >$100 verification (Slack 1775613900): check if Marcus implemented the policy. Search for follow-up in Slack thread replies or email. Marcus's thread reply (ts 1775614260) confirms acceptance and plan.
OE 13: Check calendar event for the board prep on April 28 using calendar_search_events (query: "board prep") or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: 2026-04-28, end_datetime: 2026-04-29). If found, discover cal_marcus_boardprep_0428 with attendees marcus.thorne@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com — confirm Hana Kim (hana.kim@moveops.com) is NOT on the invite. If not found (calendar data may be absent), note that no existing event was returned.
OE 14: If existing event found: edit calendar event using calendar_edit_calendar_event (event_id: cal_marcus_boardprep_0428) to add hana.kim@moveops.com to the attendees list. If no existing event found: create a new board prep event using calendar_add_calendar_event with title referencing board prep, date 2026-04-28, and attendees including hana.kim@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com.
OE 15: Look up contact emails using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova" then "Hana Kim" then "Marcus Thorne"). Confirm elena.rostova@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com.
OE 16: Send briefing email using send_email (sender: priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com, recipients: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com"]). Content: structured directive-by-directive summary — each with what Elena asked, who was responsible, what happened, what's still open. Must include specific data: ticket numbers (ENG-211, ENG-217, ENG-219, ENG-220, #318), dollar amounts ($885 exposure, $1,550 Mosaic absorption), dates, deal statuses. Flag items needing decision (BrightLoop temp coordinator, Vectral board response).
OE 17: Post heads-up in #executive using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C004, user_id: moveops_priya_chakrabarti). Content: Marcus heads-up that Elena's board prep agenda will include status updates on his open action items (vendor memo, vendor payment disputes, stipend policy).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✓Run #3Section 4 of the email covers Vectral renewal, states the 10% Preferred Partner counter-offer was sent by Mina within the 24-hour window, and explicitly flags that no confirmation from Rachel was received after the April 23 board meeting. Pending outcome clearly stated.
✗Run #4The criterion requires the email to state that the counter-offer was sent and outcome is pending. Instead, Section 6 explicitly says 'No evidence the counter-proposal was sent to Rachel' and the CRM shows 'At Risk' — the opposite of what the criterion expects.
✓Run #5Section 6 'VECTRAL RENEWAL' states Mina sent the 10% Preferred Partner counter-proposal to Rachel, Rachel board meeting was April 23, and the CRM deal is in 'at_risk' stage awaiting client decision.
✗Run #6The email describes the Vectral counter-offer as NOT sent (CRM still 'at risk', no email evidence found). The criterion requires mentioning the counter-offer was sent and outcome is pending. The agent found no evidence it was sent, which contradicts the required content.

outcome
